world.title: Trump says second round of talks with Iran could take place this weekend
world.summary: US President Donald Trump announced that a second round of talks with Iran may occur this weekend, following unsuccessful negotiations in Islamabad. He reiterated claims that Iran has agreed to surrender its "nuclear dust" and noted the effectiveness of the blockade of the Hormuz Strait. Iran's UN envoy expressed cautious optimism regarding the potential discussions.

world.title: Turkey or Pakistan could become Israel's new enemy, analyst says
world.summary: Israeli analyst Boaz Golani, in an opinion piece for Maariv, posits that Turkey or Pakistan may emerge as Israel's new adversary following the significant weakening of Iran's military capabilities due to ongoing conflicts. He describes a transformation in regional dynamics, indicating that the geopolitical landscape in the Middle East is changing, with potential implications for Israel's security strategy.

world.title: 'We are not leaving': Netanyahu says Israeli forces will hold positions in Lebanon as 10
world.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israeli forces will maintain their positions in southern Lebanon despite the ongoing 10-day ceasefire. In a video statement, he emphasized, "we are not leaving," indicating a commitment to their current military presence. This statement follows discussions with US President Donald Trump regarding the situation in the region.

business.title: Quick commerce turning to reset lane as profit push tempers growth metrics
business.summary: Quick commerce companies Blinkit and Instamart are anticipated to show slowed growth in the January-March quarter after periods of rapid expansion. Analysts and industry executives indicate that these firms are now prioritizing margin improvement and profitability over aggressive growth metrics. This shift reflects a broader trend in the sector as companies reassess their strategies for sustainable success.

business.title: Weaker rupee makes India 6th largest economy: IMF data
business.summary: India has fallen to the sixth largest economy in dollar terms, according to the latest International Monetary Fund (IMF) estimates. This shift is attributed to a weaker rupee and a change in the base year for calculating GDP. The new ranking reflects economic adjustments and highlights the impact of currency fluctuations on national economic standings.

business.title: Middle East Energy Infrastructure Damage Close to $60 Billion
business.summary: Damage to Gulf energy infrastructure due to ongoing conflicts is estimated to cost up to $58 billion for repairs, significantly exceeding previous assessments. Rystad Energy's analysis indicates that some facilities may take years to recover from the impacts of the war involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran, highlighting the extensive and lasting effects on the region's energy sector.

technology.title: OpenAI Super App Takes Shape: Codex Gets Computer Use, Browser, and Image Gen
technology.summary: OpenAI has updated its Codex desktop app, enabling it to control Mac computers, operate its own browser, and generate images. This enhancement introduces over 90 new plugins, expanding Codex's capabilities. Nearly one year after its launch, OpenAI reports that more than 3 million developers are using Codex, positioning it alongside competitors like Claude Code and OpenClaw.

technology.title: Android 17 Beta 4 has arrived: Here's what to expect
technology.summary: Android 17 Beta 4 has been released for testers, specifically for Pixel 6 and newer devices. This update follows last month’s Beta 3, which achieved platform stability. Users can expect improvements and new features as the development progresses. The final version of Android 17 is anticipated to be available in the coming months, enhancing user experience on supported devices.

technology.title: Metro 2039 Returns: Why is this the darkest Chapter yet for the iconic post-apocalyptic shooter series?
technology.summary: Metro 2039 is the latest installment in the post-apocalyptic shooter series, set in the Moscow Metro tunnels. Players face a new authoritarian regime, the Novoreich, which has united underground factions. The game focuses on themes of survival, tyranny, and freedom, featuring environmental storytelling and tense encounters. It aims to deliver a darker narrative than previous entries in the series.

sports.title: Bavarian Football Works
sports.summary: Luis Díaz, the FC Bayern winger, struggled during the Champions League match against Real Madrid but showcased his relentless work ethic. As the game neared its end, he made a significant impact, contributing to Bayern's performance. Despite earlier challenges in the match, Díaz's determination and skill have made him a key player for the team this season.

sports.title: ‘I don’t have much to say’: ‘Dejected’ Hardik Pandya after MI sink to fourth straight defeat
sports.summary: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya expressed disappointment following his team's fourth consecutive defeat, a seven-wicket loss to Punjab Kings. Pandya stated that the team is "searching for answers" as they struggle to find form. The five-time champions are facing challenges in their performance, leading to concerns about their current standing in the league.

sports.title: Ponting praised after Punjab soar to top of IPL
sports.summary: Ricky Ponting has received praise for his coaching as the Punjab Kings remain unbeaten and ascend to the top of the Indian Premier League. Under the leadership of skipper Shreyas Iyer, the team has showcased strong performances, contributing to their success. Ponting's influence is recognized by players, highlighting his effective guidance in their current winning streak.

health.title: Yet to frame a policy on SSPE, a rare disease: State tells HC
health.summary: The Maharashtra government informed the High Court that it has not yet framed a policy regarding Subacute Sclerosing Panencephalitis (SSPE), a rare neurological disorder linked to measles. This response followed a Public Interest Litigation filed by Mahadu Belkar, a father of a child affected by the disease, urging the government to take action for affected families.

health.title: Army docs save 4 from rare cardiac infection after stenting process
health.summary: A team of doctors from the Army Institute of Cardiothoracic Sciences (AICTS) in Pune successfully treated four patients with a rare and life-threatening condition known as coronary stent infection. This condition is exceptionally uncommon, with fewer than 50 reported cases globally. The successful intervention highlights the expertise of the medical team in managing complex cardiac infections.

health.title: The truth about dementia
health.summary: Neuroscientist Kumaar Bagrodia argues that “early detection” scores for dementia can be misleading. He emphasizes the need for a focus on long-term prevention strategies rather than solely relying on early diagnosis. Bagrodia critiques current marketing trends in brain-training and advocates for a comprehensive approach to dementia that prioritizes prevention to improve outcomes for individuals at risk.

science.title: Forgotten Fossil in Museum Drawer Reveals 200-Million-Year-Old Croc Ancestor with Unbelievable Bite
science.summary: A rediscovered fossil, Eosphorosuchus lacrimosa, reveals an ancient crocodile ancestor with a powerful bite and unique characteristics. This species, hidden in a museum drawer for over 70 years, enhances understanding of early crocodylomorph diversity and complexity. The findings provide valuable insights into the evolutionary history of crocodiles, reshaping perceptions of their ancestors and their ecological roles.

science.title: Ash Creeps Across Mars: Mars Express Captures a Planet in Motion [PHOTOS]
science.summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has published new images from Mars Express, showcasing the planet's changing color due to volcanic ash spreading across its bright northern plains. These images highlight the dynamic nature of Mars, capturing a significant transformation. The release marks a notable moment in the ongoing exploration and study of the Martian environment and its geological activity.

science.title: Earth from space: NASA captures mini lake carving into Canada’s 20,000-year-old ice mass
science.summary: NASA has captured an image of a mini lake forming on Canada’s ancient ice mass, which is approximately 20,000 years old. This photograph, taken from a high altitude, reveals how remote regions can showcase unique geological features. The lake's presence illustrates the ongoing changes in these ice formations, providing insights into the environmental dynamics at play in the area.

entertainment.title: Aquarius Horoscope Today, April 17, 2026: Commit to one bold goal
entertainment.summary: On April 17, 2026, Aquarians may experience tension and restlessness due to hard work. It is advised to rely on siblings for support and to concentrate on a single bold goal. Maintaining open communication with partners is essential to resolve any misunderstandings. Overall, focusing energy thoughtfully can help navigate the challenges of the day effectively.

entertainment.title: Horoscope Today: April 17, 2026
entertainment.summary: On April 17, 2026, horoscopes suggest that Gemini may encounter ancient wisdom or ruins in their life, prompting introspection. This period encourages Gemini to identify and eliminate negative influences while attempting to salvage valuable aspects. The forecast emphasizes a transformative experience, urging individuals to embrace change and growth as they navigate personal challenges and opportunities for improvement.

entertainment.title: Dhurandhar 2 Full Movie Collection: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection Day 29: Ranveer Singh starrer crosses Rs 1100 crore net mark; fails to break 'Dhurandhar' record for highest week 4 haul
entertainment.summary: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge', featuring Ranveer Singh, has surpassed the Rs 1,100 crore mark at the Indian box office after 29 days of release. Despite this achievement, it did not break the record for the highest fourth-week collection set by its predecessor, 'Dhurandhar'. The film continues to perform strongly in theaters, maintaining its popularity among audiences.
